subject: Product Management
title: Customer Empathy
description: A quick, practical guide to Customer Empathy.

hook.text: The most common PM mistake? Building for yourself instead of your actual users.

buildup.text: Customer empathy means understanding how users think, what frustrates them, and what they actually need.

discovery.text: Great PMs spend time with users regularly: interviews, support ticket reviews, usability tests, ride-alongs.

twist.text: Empathy isn't agreement. Understanding why a user wants something doesn't mean you should build it.

climax.text: The PM's job is to be the user's voice in the room — even when the room doesn't want to hear it.

punchline.text: Build for the user in front of you, not the one in your head.

quiz.question: Why is customer empathy critical for PMs?
options: It prevents building for yourself instead of actual users
It means always building what users ask for
It only matters for consumer products
